title: Electrolysis electrode featuring metal-doped nanotube array and methods of manufacture and using same
abstract: An electrolysis electrode includes a metal-doped array of nanotubes formed on a substrate. The nanotube array (NTA) may be a stabilized metal-doped black TiO2 NTA formed on a titanium substrate, and the metal dopant may include any suitable metal, for example, cobalt. The metal dopant improves the reactivity of the electrode and enhances its service life. The metal-doped NTA electrode may provide improved chlorine evolution and/or oxygen evolution activity for electrochemical wastewater treatment. The electrode may also be useful for water splitting applications. Increasing the loading of the metal dopant may lead to the formation of a metal oxide layer on top of the NTA, which improves oxygen evolution reaction (OER) overpotential.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An electrolysis electrode, comprising: a substrate; and a metal-doped nanotube array contacting the substrate. 2. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the nanotube array comprises Ti0 2 . 3. The electrode of claim 2, wherein the nanotube array comprises black Ti0 2 . 4. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the doping metal is selected from the group consisting of nickel, iron, cobalt, cerium, manganese, vanadium, tin, lead, a noble metal, and any combination of the foregoing. 5. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the metal-doped nanotube array includes a cobalt-doped black T1O 2 nanotube array. 6. The electrode of claim 1, further comprising a metal oxide film on top of the nanotube array. 7. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the metal included in the metal-doped nanotube array has a loading of less than 5 Î¼Î¹ÏÎ¹Î¿Î/Î±ÏÎ¹ 2 . 8. The electrode of claim 7, wherein the metal included in the metal-doped nanotube array has a loading between about 0.17 Î¼Î¹ÏÎ¹Î¿Î/Î±ÏÎ¹ 2 and 0.54 Î¼Î¹ÏÎ¹Î¿Î/Î±ÏÎ¹ 2 . 9. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the amount of metal included in the metal-doped nanotube array is tuned to a predetermined amount based on the application of the electrode. 10. The electrode of claim 1, wherein the substrate includes titanium. 11. A water processing system, comprising: an electrode configured to be, at least in part, in direct contact with water, the electrode including a substrate, and a metal-doped nanotube array contacting the substrate. 12. The system of claim 11, further comprising a second electrode. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the second electrode includes a substrate, and a metal-doped nanotube array contacting the substrate. 14. The system of claim 11, further comprising: a voltage source connected to the electrode and a second electrode configured to contact the water; wherein the voltage source is configured to switch polarity at a predetermined frequency so that the electrode operates as either an anode or a cathode based on the polarity of the voltage source. 15. The system of claim 11, wherein the metal-doped nanotube array is a cobalt-doped black T1O 2 nanotube array formed on the substrate, wherein the substrate includes titanium. 16. A method of manufacturing an electrolysis electrode, comprising : forming an amorphous nanotube array on a substrate by a first anodization of the substrate; subjecting the amorphous nanotube array and substrate to a second anodization; applying a metal dopant solution to the amorphous nanotube array, whereby producing a metal-doped amorphous nanotube array; and annealing the metal-doped amorphous nanotube array in a gas stream. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein applying a metal dopant includes : 

 dipping the amorphous nanotube array into the metal dopant solution for a predetermined amount of time. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the metal dopant solution includes a metal nitrate salt dissolved in ethanol, wherein the metal nitrate salt is selected from the group consisting of Co(N0 3 ) 2 , Fe(N0 3 ) 3 , Ni(N0 3 ) 2 , Mn(N0 3 ) 2 , and Ce(N0 3 ) 2 . 19. The method of claim 16, wherein the gas stream includes hydrogen and argon. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein the substrate includes titanium and amorphous nanotube array includes Ti0 2 .
